refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall analyze the worksite area by identifying terrain features, detecting obstacles, and representing the work-zone accurately.
refined_fr.rationale: Breaking down the broad worksite mapping requirement into specific sub-functions ensures clarity and testability, addressing terrain, obstacles, and work-zone representation distinctly.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify through simulation and field tests that terrain data is captured correctly, obstacles are detected with required accuracy, and the work-zone is represented in the system map as per specifications.
sub_frs: FR1: Identify and characterize terrain features of the worksite.
FR2: Detect and locate obstacles within the worksite area.
FR3: Represent the work-zone boundaries and relevant site features in the worksite model.

candidate_dps: DP1: Use LIDAR and camera sensor fusion for terrain and obstacle detection.
DP2: Employ a centralized mapping algorithm to integrate sensor data into a unified worksite model.
DP3: Implement a geospatial database to store and manage work-zone representations.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Use LIDAR and camera sensor fusion for terrain and obstacle detection.

potential_sources: Sensor data integration tightly coupled with mapping algorithm.
Obstacle detection depends on terrain feature identification.
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP logically supports the FR with manageable coupling; sensor fusion is a well-established approach minimizing cross-domain dependencies.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability of high-fidelity LIDAR and camera sensors.
Risk of sensor data noise affecting accuracy.
Risk of integration complexity between sensor fusion and mapping modules.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider modularizing sensor fusion and mapping algorithms to reduce integration complexity.

im_boundary.name: Vehicle
im_boundary.scope: Physical and embedded systems responsible for sensor data acquisition and initial processing.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Transmits processed terrain and obstacle data to NGHE Solution for further mapping and planning.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Supports data exchange between Vehicle and NGHE Solution modules.

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment of sensor data acquisition and initial processing; low diffusion as data is output via Telemetry.
Contains mapping and planning functions but less suitable for raw sensor processing.
Minimal relevance to sensor data processing; mostly consumes processed data.
Primarily concerned with safety alerts, not mapping.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Sensor data acquisition and initial processing are best contained within the Vehicle module to minimize data diffusion and maintain real-time processing capabilities.
containment_risks: Potential latency in transmitting processed data to NGHE Solution for mapping.
Risk of overloading Vehicle processing resources.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Evaluate offloading some processing to NGHE Solution if Vehicle resource constraints arise.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP (sensor fusion) directly addresses the refined FR sub-functions with clear mapping and manageable coupling.

diffusion_risks: Data transmission latency between Vehicle and NGHE Solution.
Potential duplication of processing if not well partitioned.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Vehicle module is appropriate for sensor data processing; interfaces are well-defined and limited.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed via integrated system tests involving sensor data acquisition and mapping output validation.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of sensor processing to Vehicle and mapping to NGHE Solution ensures ownership and responsibility.

recommended_revisions: Monitor processing load on Vehicle to avoid performance bottlenecks.
Define clear interface protocols for Telemetry and Network Link to ensure data integrity.

decision.justification: While the decomposition and assignments are sound, the high safety criticality and potential risks in data processing latency and resource constraints warrant further detailed decomposition and risk mitigation at the next level.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Identify and characterize terrain features of the worksite.
FR2: Detect and locate obstacles within the worksite area.
FR3: Represent the work-zone boundaries and relevant site features in the worksite model.

revise.why: Refine sensor fusion and mapping algorithms to address integration complexity and performance risks.

assumptions: High-fidelity sensors are available and reliable.
Vehicle has sufficient processing capability for initial sensor data processing.
open_risks: Latency in data transmission affecting real-time mapping.
Resource constraints on Vehicle processing units.
Potential inaccuracies due to sensor noise.
evidence_needed: Performance benchmarks of sensor fusion algorithms on Vehicle hardware.
Field test results validating obstacle detection accuracy.
